steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank separates 'made several innovations to the steel drum' (an independent clause) from a three-item list: wheels, a cover, and amplification. We need punctuation that correctly introduces that list.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — comma: A comma treats the drum as one of four parallel items, which distorts the sentence's meaning. ✗
  • Choice B — colon: A colon follows an independent clause and introduces a list that elaborates on it — exactly what is needed here. ✓
  • Choice C — no punctuation: Without punctuation, the relationship between the clause and the list is unclear. ✗
  • Choice D — semicolon: A semicolon joins two independent clauses; the list that follows is not an independent clause. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
